message:
ft-xbnl0.2.3: extend ambient-Cx inheritance to mpsc_recv_state and IpcServer::bind_with_permissions

Two more legacy entry points still unconditionally forged a fresh
request-rooted Cx on the asupersync side instead of inheriting an
ambient one. Bring them into line with the existing pattern.

Changes in crates/frankenterm-core/src/ipc.rs:

* `mpsc_recv_state` (the async helper that awaits a recv/close result
  on an mpsc receiver):
  - Asupersync branch now uses
    `crate::cx::Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(crate::cx::for_request)`
    before delegating to `mpsc_recv_state_with_cx`.
  - Without this, every recv poll inside a handler task manufactures
    a new request Cx, so when the parent task is cancelled the
    child poll's Cx doesn't reflect it, and the recv outlives
    the intended cancellation window.

* `IpcServer::bind_with_permissions`:
  - The asupersync branch now fetches an ambient Cx (or falls back
    to a fresh one) and immediately delegates to
    `Self::bind_with_permissions_with_cx(&cx, socket_path, permissions)`.
    This is the same pattern the other legacy entry points already
    use: keep a single Cx for the bind operation rather than letting
    the pre-bind stale-socket removal, bind, and metadata setup each
    spin up their own request Cx under asupersync.
  - The `cfg(not(feature = "asupersync-runtime"))` branch keeps the
    original inline bind implementation unchanged (remove stale
    socket file → bind → return `IpcServer { socket_path, listener }`).

No production-side behavior change on tokio. On asupersync, these two
paths now participate in the caller's cancellation chain instead of
siloing themselves.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
